examples.title: TCP Client Connection (Three-Way Handshake)
examples.description: Pseudocode showing client-side TCP connection establishment
examples.explanation: The client initiates TCP connection by creating a socket and calling connect(), which sends SYN. After receiving SYN-ACK from server, client sends ACK completing the three-way handshake.

examples.title: TCP Server Connection (Passive Open)
examples.description: Pseudocode showing server-side TCP socket creation and listening
examples.explanation: Server creates socket, binds to port, listens for connections. The accept() call blocks until a client connects, completing the three-way handshake and returning a new socket for communication.

examples.title: TCP Data Transfer with Flow Control
examples.description: Pseudocode showing reliable data transfer with flow control using sliding window
examples.code:
TCP_RELIABLE_SEND(socket, data)
BEGIN
 window_size = get_receive_window(socket)
 sent_unacked = 0
 base = current_sequence_number
 
 WHILE data_remaining > 0 DO
 // Wait if window is full
 WHILE sent_unacked >= window_size DO
 wait_for_ack()
 sent_unacked = update_window(base)
 END WHILE
 
 // Send segment
 segment = create_segment(seq=base+sent_unacked, data)
 send(socket, segment)
 sent_unacked = sent_unacked + segment_size
 
 // Start timer for oldest unacked segment
 IF sent_unacked == 1 THEN
 start_retransmission_timer()
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Wait for all acknowledgments
 wait_until_all_acked()
END

TCP_RECEIVE_AND_ACK(socket)
BEGIN
 WHILE TRUE DO
 segment = receive(socket)
 
 IF checksum_valid(segment) THEN
 // Send cumulative ACK
 ack_num = expected_sequence_number
 send(socket, ACK + ack=ack_num)
 
 // Update receive window
 window_size = available_buffer_space
 send(socket, window=window_size)
 ELSE
 // Discard corrupted segment
 // Do not send ACK (sender will retransmit)
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Shows how TCP ensures reliability through acknowledgments and flow control through window size. Sender waits when window is full; receiver ACKs valid segments and advertises available buffer space.

examples.title: TCP Connection Termination
examples.description: Pseudocode showing the four-way handshake for connection termination
examples.explanation: Shows both client and server sides of connection termination. Client initiates with FIN, waits for server's FIN, then sends final ACK. TIME_WAIT ensures delayed segments are handled properly.
